title: Zinnia flavicoma
intro: The Zinnia flavicoma, also known as the Mexican zinnia or golden zinnia, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States, characterized by its bright yellow or orange flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering relief from various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory infections, thanks to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Zinnia flavicoma include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat coughs, sore throats, and other respiratory ailments.
taxonomy: Zinnia flavicoma is classified as a member of the Asteraceae family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its flower structure and leaf arrangement. The Asteraceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Zinnia flavicoma is classified within the subfamily Heliantoideae. The species itself is classified within the genus Zinnia, which is native to the Americas. Zinnia flavicoma is a relatively rare and endemic species.
